text.title: Extreme Programming Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Extreme Programming methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every code change MUST follow red-green-refactor: failing test → minimum code → green → refactor while green; coding-without-test is forbidden on hot paths.
rule.rationale: Without a failing test first, code lacks a verification contract; refactoring after the fact is unsafe without baseline tests.

rule.statement: Engineers MUST integrate to trunk at least daily; PR merges blocked until CI is green; long-lived branches >3 days are forbidden.

rationale.source: DevOps Research and Assessment; Continuous Delivery (Humble & Farley)

rule.rationale: Long branches drift; trunk-based discipline catches integration issues at minute scale, not week scale.

rule.statement: Pair programming (or AI-pair for solos) MUST happen on >50% of code; pairs rotate every <=2 days to spread knowledge.
rule.rationale: Solo coding accumulates bus-factor risk and reduces design discussion; rotation distributes context across the team.

rule.statement: Refactoring MUST happen with green tests AND in the same session as the green commit; deferred refactor is forbidden as standard practice.
rule.rationale: Deferred refactor accumulates; refactor-while-green keeps the codebase healthy without big-bang efforts.

rule.statement: Team MUST have a named customer or proxy available with <24h response SLA on business questions; absent customer is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a customer, the team guesses at requirements; daily questions accumulate misalignment.

rule.statement: Sustained overtime is forbidden; weekly hours MUST stay within 40 + occasional surge; chronic overtime is a defect.
rule.rationale: Overtime erodes the discipline that XP requires; bug rates climb, refactor is skipped, tests are dropped.

rule.statement: Production releases MUST happen at <=2 week cadence at most; longer release cycles are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Long cycles compound risk and delay feedback; short cycles surface integration issues early.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
